exercises.title: Which constraint catches which input?
exercises.scenario: Bean Validation constraints each catch specific invalid inputs.
exercises.instructions:
Match each invalid input to the constraint that would catch it:

Inputs:
A. name = ""  (empty string)
B. price = -50
C. qty = 1001, where @Max(1000) applied
D. email = "not-an-email"
E. sku = "abc" where @Pattern(regexp="^[A-Z]{3}$")

Constraints: @NotBlank, @Positive, @Max, @Email, @Pattern

exercises.title: Annotate a request DTO
exercises.scenario: Bean Validation annotations declare the rules.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the annotations to validate this order request.

blanks.hint: Rejects null, empty, and whitespace-only strings
Number must be strictly greater than 0

hints: @NotBlank for strings; @Positive for any positive number

exercises.title: Minimal @ControllerAdvice validation handler
exercises.scenario: One @RestControllerAdvice handles all validation errors globally.
exercises.instructions: Write a `GlobalExceptionHandler` with @RestControllerAdvice that handles MethodArgumentNotValidException and returns a Map<String, String> of field → message with 400 status.

exercises.title: Custom ResourceNotFoundException
exercises.scenario: Custom exceptions map business errors to HTTP status codes.
exercises.instructions:
Write:
1. `ResourceNotFoundException` extending RuntimeException with `(String resource, String id)` constructor
2. `DuplicateResourceException` extending RuntimeException
3. @ExceptionHandler for each in @RestControllerAdvice — 404 and 409 respectively
hints: RuntimeException: no checked exception declaration needed

exercises.title: @Valid in controller — trigger validation
exercises.scenario: @Valid must be on the parameter, not just on the DTO fields.
exercises.instructions:
Write a complete controller method `createProduct(@RequestBody ProductRequest req)` that:
1. Has `@Valid` to trigger validation
2. Returns 201 Created with saved product on success
3. Show that without @Valid the constraints are ignored
hints: Without @Valid: annotations on the record are read but never enforced

exercises.title: Complete error handler — all 4 exception types
exercises.scenario: Production @RestControllerAdvice covers validation, not-found, conflict, and generic errors.
hints: Catch-all: return generic message, never ex.getMessage()

exercises.title: @WebMvcTest — test validation response
exercises.scenario: @WebMvcTest tests the HTTP layer with MockMvc.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @WebMvcTest that:
1. POSTs a product with blank name and negative price
2. Asserts 400 status
3. Asserts response body contains 'name' and 'price' error keys

Use MockMvc and ObjectMapper.
hints: jsonPath('$.name').exists() checks the field exists in response JSON

exercises.title: Test 404 and 409 error responses
exercises.scenario: @WebMvcTest for not-found and duplicate error scenarios.
exercises.instructions:
Write @WebMvcTest tests for:
1. GET /api/products/P999 → service throws ResourceNotFoundException → 404 with error key
2. POST with duplicate name → service throws DuplicateResourceException → 409 with error key

exercises.title: @Pattern and @Email validation
exercises.instructions:
Write a `UserRegistrationRequest` with:
- email: @NotBlank + @Email
- username: @NotBlank + @Size(min=3, max=20) + @Pattern(^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$)
- age: @Min(18) + @Max(120)
- password: @NotBlank + @Size(min=8)

Then write @WebMvcTest testing invalid email and underage user.
hints: @Min(18) on int age: rejects any age < 18

exercises.title: Exception handler specificity
exercises.expected_solution:
handleIllegalState fires → HTTP 422 'illegal state'
Reason: IllegalStateException is more specific than RuntimeException or Exception

test_cases.expected:
handleIllegalState fires → HTTP 422 'illegal state'
Reason: IllegalStateException is more specific than RuntimeException or Exception

hints: Spring picks the closest match in the exception hierarchy

exercises.title: Structured ErrorResponse record
exercises.scenario: A consistent error response format improves API usability.
exercises.instructions: Create an `ErrorResponse` record with status, error, message, path, timestamp fields. Update GlobalExceptionHandler to use it for all handlers. Test with @WebMvcTest that the JSON response has all fields.
exercises.starter_code:
import java.time.*;
// ErrorResponse record + updated GlobalExceptionHandler
hints: HttpServletRequest req as parameter: Spring injects the current request
req.getRequestURI() gives the path that was called

exercises.title: Payment validation DTO — Razorpay style
exercises.scenario: Real-world payment API has strict validation requirements.
exercises.instructions:
Build a `PaymentRequest` record with validation:
- amount: @Positive @Min(100) (minimum ₹1 = 100 paise) @Max(10000000)
- currency: @NotBlank @Pattern(INR|USD|EUR) with message
- orderId: @NotBlank @Size(max=40)
- description: @Size(max=255) (optional — no @NotBlank)

Then write @WebMvcTest tests for: valid request (201), amount too small (400), invalid currency (400).
hints: @Min(100) and @Positive both required — @Min(100) implies > 0 for positive values
description has no @NotBlank: optional field, validated only if present

exercises.title: Full validation + exception integration test
exercises.scenario: Complete validation chain from controller to error response.
exercises.instructions:
Build and test a full `UserController` with:
- UserRegistrationRequest (email, username, age, password with constraints)
- UserService (creates user, throws DuplicateResourceException if email exists)
- GlobalExceptionHandler
- @WebMvcTest with 4 tests:
  1. Valid registration → 201
  2. Invalid email → 400 with email error key
  3. Underage → 400 with age error key
  4. Duplicate email → 409 with error message
hints: @MockBean UserService: mock the service in the test
All 4 tests target different error scenarios

exercises.title: @Validated on controller — path variable validation
exercises.instructions:
Add @Validated to the controller class and validate:
- @PathVariable id: @Pattern(regexp='P\\d{3}', message='ID must be P + 3 digits')
- @RequestParam limit: @Min(1) @Max(100)

Test with @WebMvcTest that invalid path variable returns 400.
hints: @Validated on class level enables method-level parameter validation

exercises.title: Complete product API with validation + exceptions — Module 13.4 synthesis
exercises.scenario: Production-ready product API with full validation and error handling.
exercises.instructions:
Build the complete production-ready product API:
- ProductRequest (5 constrained fields)
- Product @Entity
- ProductService (@Transactional, throws ResourceNotFoundException and DuplicateResourceException)
- ProductController (GET all, GET/{id}, POST with @Valid, PUT/{id}, DELETE/{id})
- GlobalExceptionHandler (4 handlers)
- @WebMvcTest with 6 tests covering: happy path, validation failure, not found, duplicate, delete success, delete not found
hints: findByIdOrThrow: service method that throws ResourceNotFoundException if not found
All 6 test cases target different HTTP scenarios
